en_title: The Mario 64 Bounty That Took 30 Years to Settle
cn_title: IGN三十年前悬赏寻找《马力欧64》里的路易吉，现在该还债了
subtitle: L真的存在
opus_summary: IGN曾在1996年悬赏100美元(约合人民币679元)，要求玩家证明路易吉存在于《超级马力欧64》中。三十年后，Gigaleak泄露的源码和材质终于让这桩旧案有了答案。

paragraphs.en: If you were gaming back in 1996, there’s a good chance you heard a rumour. Maybe you saw it in a magazine, or it was whispered to you in the schoolyard, but either way the gossip was consistent: Luigi was in Mario 64 .
paragraphs.cn: 如果你在1996年玩游戏，很可能听过一个传言。也许你在杂志上看过，也许是在校园操场上听人小声说起，但无论如何，八卦内容都很一致：路易吉在《马力欧64》里。

paragraphs.en: The rumour was relentless, and the appetite for Mario’s brother grew by the day. IGN, however, struggled to believe it was true. In an effort to dispel the myth once and for all, in 1996 we posted a bounty. The message was simple: Prove Luigi is in the game and we will pay you $100.
paragraphs.cn: 这个传言挥之不去，而玩家对马力欧兄弟的渴望也与日俱增。不过，IGN很难相信这是真的。为了彻底打破这个神话，我们在1996年发布了一项悬赏。信息很简单：证明路易吉在游戏里，我们就付你100美元(约合人民币679元)。

paragraphs.en: Countless tried, none succeeded. And after 24 years people moved on, forgetting they’d ever cared about the missing plumber being in the iconic 3D platformer.
paragraphs.cn: 无数人尝试过，没有人成功。24年之后，人们继续向前走，忘记自己曾经在意过这位失踪水管工是否存在于这款标志性3D平台游戏中。

paragraphs.en: But then, seemingly out of nowhere, Nintendo's source code was exposed to the world, and buried in there was a lead. Luigi, it appeared, was there, hidden in a place where nobody could look. But now, thanks to a data breach dubbed the “Gigaleak” and some clever sleuthing, he was finally exposed to the world.
paragraphs.cn: 但随后，仿佛凭空发生一样，任天堂源码暴露在世人面前，里面埋着一条线索。看起来，路易吉确实在那里，藏在一个没人能看的地方。而现在，得益于一场被称为「Gigaleak」的数据泄露，以及一些聪明的追查，他终于暴露在世界面前。

paragraphs.en: So now, 30 years later, it’s time to finish this. Let’s settle this bounty once and for all.
paragraphs.cn: 所以现在，30年后，是时候把这件事做完了。让我们一次性了结这笔悬赏。

paragraphs.en: Before Super Mario 64 arrived in 1996, it was inconceivable that Mario’s first jump to three dimensions would happen without his brother. Together, they formed the titular Mario Bros we’d seen together since 1985. But the inconceivable happened – Mario 64 was a solo adventure for Nintendo’s original jumping man. That wasn’t the original plan, though. In an interview for the game’s official Japanese strategy guide , creator Shigeru Miyamoto explained that the team had been forced to omit Luigi, stating that “ultimately, due to memory issues, we had to take him out.”
paragraphs.cn: 在《超级马力欧64》于1996年到来之前，人们很难想象马力欧第一次跳进三维世界时会没有他的兄弟。自1985年以来，他们一起构成了我们所熟悉的标题中的Mario Bros。但难以想象的事情还是发生了：《马力欧64》成了任天堂原初跳跃男的单人冒险。不过，这并不是最初计划。在游戏官方日本攻略本的一次采访中，创作者宫本茂解释说，团队被迫删掉路易吉，称「最终，由于内存问题，我们不得不把他拿掉。」

paragraphs.en: So, Luigi was never in Mario 64. Open and shut case, you’d assume. But that didn’t stop the rumours spreading like wildfire.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，路易吉从来不在《马力欧64》里。你可能会以为案子到此结束。但这并没有阻止传言像野火一样蔓延。

paragraphs.en: This hearsay about Luigi wasn’t completely unfounded. Its origins all stemmed from a simple, practically unreadable sign in the courtyard of Peach’s castle.
paragraphs.cn: 关于路易吉的传闻并非完全没有根据。它的源头都来自桃花公主城堡庭院里一块简单到几乎无法辨认的牌子。

paragraphs.en: “The text on the sign, I'm pretty sure, was just nonsense,” says Phillips. “But people thought it said: L is real 2401."
paragraphs.cn: Phillips说：「我很确定，牌子上的文字只是无意义内容。但人们以为它写着：L is real 2401。」

paragraphs.en: “People from that took that, yes, Luigi is in the game, and you had to do something to find him, whether that was collect 2,401 stars or jump 2,401 times in a specific spot. But [fans assumed] it was a message from Shigeru Miyamoto himself, saying that Luigi was waiting somewhere for you to go find.”
paragraphs.cn: 「人们由此认为，是的，路易吉在游戏里，而你必须做些什么才能找到他。可能是收集2401颗星，也可能是在某个特定地点跳2401次。但粉丝认为，这是宫本茂本人留下的信息，说路易吉就在某处等你去找。」

paragraphs.en: Super Mario 64 was like nothing else on the market in 1996, defining not only the rules for all 3D platformers but also quickly establishing itself as one of the greatest video games of all time. But perhaps where it stood out the most, at least to a legion of Luigi obsessives, was the sheer volume of secrets you could find.
paragraphs.cn: 1996年，《超级马力欧64》和市场上的任何东西都不一样。它不仅定义了所有3D平台游戏的规则，也迅速确立了自己作为史上最伟大电子游戏之一的地位。但至少对一大批迷恋路易吉的人来说，它最突出的地方也许是你能找到的秘密数量。

paragraphs.en: “It's a remarkable game. I was in awe,” shares IGN co-founder Douglass C. Perry . “There were so many little hidden things throughout the game that made it so much fun to explore. The sense of discovery was one of the things that made it so remarkable and long-lasting. There are so many secrets that it made you feel like Luigi could be in there. “
paragraphs.cn: IGN联合创始人Douglass C. Perry分享说：「这是一款非凡的游戏。我当时叹为观止。游戏里到处都有那么多隐藏的小东西，让探索变得非常有趣。发现感正是让它如此非凡、如此长久的原因之一。它有太多秘密，让你觉得路易吉真的可能藏在里面。」

paragraphs.en: But despite the community's best efforts, Luigi was nowhere to be found. After collecting all 120 stars, players were rewarded with a secret Yoshi hidden right at the start of the game. But no matter how hard they looked, there was no trace of the moustachioed brother. He would eventually arrive in the Nintendo DS port of the game, but many fans were still convinced he was in the original.
paragraphs.cn: 但尽管社区竭尽全力，路易吉仍然无处可寻。收集全部120颗星后，玩家会获得隐藏在游戏开头位置的秘密耀西作为奖励。但无论他们怎么寻找，都找不到这位留着小胡子的兄弟的踪迹。路易吉最终会在游戏的任天堂DS移植版中登场，但许多粉丝仍然确信他就在原版里。

paragraphs.en: “I would love for Luigi to truly be in the game,” says Perry. I think that would be amazing, but that doesn't mean that he's there.”
paragraphs.cn: Perry说：「我当然希望路易吉真的在游戏里。我觉得那会很惊人，但这并不意味着他就在那里。」

paragraphs.en: The audience, armed with a hidden message, was undeterred. Luigi had to be in the game, and ‘L is Real 2401’ was the clue they needed. At the same time, a burgeoning website called N64.com launched and was quickly inundated with questions about the missing plumber. In an effort to dispel the myth once and for all, Douglass C. Perry, who was a young editor at the site, decided to put his money where his mouth was and offer a $100 bounty to anyone who could solve this mystery . It was a call to arms that he’d very quickly regret…
paragraphs.cn: 拥有隐藏信息的玩家并未退缩。路易吉必须在游戏里，而「L is Real 2401」就是他们需要的线索。与此同时，一个名为N64.com的新兴网站上线，并很快被关于这位失踪水管工的问题淹没。为了彻底打破这个神话，当时还是该网站年轻编辑的Douglass C. Perry决定拿钱说话，向任何能解开这个谜团的人提供100美元(约合人民币679元)悬赏。这是一次号召行动，而他很快就会后悔……

paragraphs.en: The staff of N64.com , which would later change its name to IGN64 and then just IGN, thought nothing of their bounty story. It was just one article towards a daily quota, and the assumption was it would be forgotten in less time than it took to post it…
paragraphs.cn: N64.com的员工们并没有把这篇悬赏文章当回事。这个网站后来会改名为IGN64，再后来变成IGN。对他们来说，这只是日常发稿配额中的一篇文章，大家以为它会在比发布它还短的时间内被遗忘……

paragraphs.en: "Hey, Doug, can you write five stories a day?" recalls Perry. “I was like, ’Yeah, I can do it,’ blindly accepting the challenge. And so we were constantly looking for things to write. And of course, these emails started coming in about Luigi, and we were like, ‘He's not in the game, we would've found him by now,’ but the emails were relentless. Finally, I was like, well, fine, let's just put the challenge out there for players to find Luigi. Because if he's in there, someone's going to find it.
paragraphs.cn: Perry回忆说：「『嘿，Doug，你一天能写五篇吗？』我当时就像，『没问题，我能做到』，盲目接受了挑战。所以我们一直在找东西写。当然，关于路易吉的邮件开始涌进来，我们就想，『他不在游戏里，如果在的话我们早就找到了。』但邮件没完没了。最后我就说，好吧，行，那就把挑战发出去，让玩家去找路易吉。因为如果他在里面，总有人会找到。」

paragraphs.en: “I said, ’If you can find Luigi in the game, show us the steps and pictures, and we'll reward you.’ Well, that was just the biggest mistake I think I'd ever made…”
paragraphs.cn: 「我说，『如果你能在游戏里找到路易吉，向我们展示步骤和图片，我们会奖励你。』结果那大概是我犯过的最大错误……」

paragraphs.en: In 1996, the internet was in its infancy, so the idea of the answer being a quick Google search away was a distant, warp-pipe dream. In the late ‘90s, rumours such as the ability to revive Aerith in Final Fantasy 7 , or a secret code to reduce Lara Croft to her bare pixels, were prevalent in schoolyards. Fans laboured over these urban myths, waiting months at a time for them to be either solved or dispelled by magazines.
paragraphs.cn: 1996年，互联网还处于婴儿期，所以答案只要快速Google一下就能找到这种想法，还只是遥远的水管梦。90年代后期，诸如能让《最终幻想7》里的爱丽丝复活，或有秘密代码能把劳拉·克劳馥变成裸像素之类的传闻，在校园里非常流行。粉丝们围着这些都市传说苦苦钻研，一次等待几个月，等杂志来解决或辟谣。

paragraphs.en: The internet, though, had begun to speed up this process, and with $100 on the line, the audience wasn’t going to wait patiently for their answers.
paragraphs.cn: 不过，互联网已经开始加速这个过程，而在100美元(约合人民币679元)悬赏摆在面前时，玩家不会耐心等待答案。

paragraphs.en: “We had mountains of emails coming in, just relentless,” shares Perry. “I hadn't really thought past the challenge part. I was like, ‘Oh, I have to follow all these emails, instructions and go through every single one of these challenges to find out if he's actually in there.’
paragraphs.cn: Perry分享说：「我们收到堆积如山的邮件，完全没完没了。我根本没想过挑战发出去之后的部分。我当时才意识到，『哦，我得跟进所有这些邮件、说明，然后逐一检查这些挑战，弄清楚他到底在不在里面。』」

paragraphs.en: “It was an enormous amount of work, and I didn’t think he was in there. None of them worked!”
paragraphs.cn: 「那是巨量工作，而我并不认为他在里面。那些方法一个都没用！」

paragraphs.en: The staff at N64.com were confident Luigi wasn’t in the game, but others still desperately sought confirmation. Two years later, in response to an ardent fan, Nintendo of America sent a letter confirming that ‘L is Real’ was simply a joke placed by developers, although evidence of that letter wouldn’t make its way onto gaming forums until many years later , and that’s assuming it was even legitimate. So, for most, true confirmation was still decades away.
paragraphs.cn: N64.com的员工确信路易吉不在游戏中，但其他人仍然迫切想得到确认。两年后，任天堂美国在回复一名热心粉丝时寄出信件，确认「L is Real」只是开发者放进去的玩笑。不过，这封信的证据要到很多年后才出现在游戏论坛上，而且前提是它真的合法可信。所以，对大多数人来说，真正的确认仍然要等几十年。

paragraphs.en: “I was reading Nintendo Power and all the magazines, and there was nothing,” says Perry. “ I literally tried a hundred different ways to unlock Luigi, and none of them worked, but it didn't stop the emails from coming.”
paragraphs.cn: Perry说：「我当时在读《Nintendo Power》和所有杂志，什么都没有。我真的尝试过一百种不同方法来解锁路易吉，没有一种成功，但这并没有阻止邮件继续涌来。」

paragraphs.en: What started out as a fun article had spiralled out of control with no end in sight. Miyamoto had confirmed he wasn’t there, Nintendo of America confirmed he wasn’t there, and not a single person could confirm to IGN they’d ever had a whiff of Luigi. All they had was ‘L is Real 2401’.
paragraphs.cn: 一篇有趣文章开始失控，而且看不到尽头。宫本茂确认他不在那里，任天堂美国确认他不在那里，也没有任何一个人能向IGN证明自己闻到过哪怕一丝路易吉的气味。他们拥有的只有「L is Real 2401」。

paragraphs.en: That was until 24 years and 01 month later…
paragraphs.cn: 直到24年又01个月之后……

paragraphs.en: In July 2020, details of secret Nintendo development data began to surface . What started off as a trickle of information quickly spiralled into one of the biggest video game leaks in history.
paragraphs.cn: 2020年7月，任天堂秘密开发数据的细节开始浮出水面。最初只是零星信息，很快演变成电子游戏史上最大泄露事件之一。

paragraphs.en: “I thought it was a fake at first, but seemingly it was legit.”
paragraphs.cn: 「我一开始以为是假的，但看起来它是真的。」

paragraphs.en: Enormous amounts of development information began pouring out as eager fans mined the hacked data, desperate to learn more about Nintendo. Fans were quick to dub this enormous data breach the “Gigaleak” as they sifted through mountains of designs, prototypes and source material that Nintendo never intended to see the light of day.
paragraphs.cn: 随着热切粉丝挖掘被黑数据，大量开发资料开始涌出。他们急切地想了解更多任天堂信息。粉丝很快把这场巨大数据泄露称为「Gigaleak」，一边筛查成山的设计、原型和源码材料，这些东西原本任天堂从没打算让它们见光。

paragraphs.en: One person whose curiosity was piqued by the news was an anonymous software engineer/visual artist (and passionate Super Mario 64 fan) known only as GlitchyPSI . With potentially massive Mario revelations ready for the taking, they were keen to dive into the Gigaleak to see what they could discover.
paragraphs.cn: 一位被这条新闻勾起好奇心的人，是一名只以GlitchyPSI为名的匿名软件工程师/视觉艺术家，也是热情的《超级马力欧64》粉丝。面对可能存在的巨大马力欧发现，他们急于钻进Gigaleak，看自己能发现什么。

paragraphs.en: “It was like finding some kind of treasure, even though there was questionable legality over it,” explains Glitchy. “It was kind of like, ’Damn, this is kind of scary.’”
paragraphs.cn: Glitchy解释说：「那就像发现某种宝藏，尽管它在法律层面有问题。感觉有点像，『该死，这有点吓人。』」

paragraphs.en: “There were a lot of comments by people, I believe, who actually work on this stuff. I believe a Starfox developer said something about disrespecting people's work by doing this. There was a lot of fear, but in my circles, there was also a lot of excitement because people wanted to see the assets themselves.”
paragraphs.cn: 「有很多评论来自我相信真正做这些东西的人。我记得一名《星际火狐》开发者说，这样做是不尊重人们的工作。那里有很多恐惧，但在我的圈子里，也有很多兴奋，因为大家想亲眼看到那些素材。」

paragraphs.en: “I sort of adhere to the argument that this stuff is just so interesting and important for fans of Nintendo games and for fans of video games as a medium,” says Phillips. “It's a part of our culture. And I think the more we know about it, the better, whether Nintendo agrees with that or not.”
paragraphs.cn: Phillips说：「我有点认同这样的论点：这些东西对任天堂游戏粉丝、对把电子游戏视为一种媒介的粉丝来说，都太有趣也太重要了。它是我们文化的一部分。我认为我们越了解它越好，不管任天堂是否同意。」

paragraphs.en: “The files were 'encrypted’,” explains Glitchy. “So you had to run a tool to get to the contents of the actual files as they were laid out in the file system, but you can imagine what else I found in there.”
paragraphs.cn: Glitchy解释说：「那些文件是『加密』的。所以你必须运行一个工具，才能看到文件系统里实际排列的文件内容，但你可以想象我还在里面发现了什么。」

paragraphs.en: What was discovered was a treasure trove of unreleased Nintendo content. There were prototypes for games like Yoshi’s Island, Super Mario Kart, and Star Fox 2. Mini-games that never saw the light of day, unused art assets, and location and character beta designs.
paragraphs.cn: 被发现的是一座未发布任天堂内容的宝库。里面有《耀西岛》《超级马力欧卡丁车》和《星际火狐2》等游戏的原型，还有从未见光的小游戏、未使用美术素材，以及地点和角色的测试设计。

paragraphs.en: “I was refreshing that page, trying to see the latest stuff pieced together from it,” shares Phillips. “You never ever see how the product is made. And so that was what was so exciting about the Gigleak: to see the work-in-progress versions, the things that were left on the cutting room floor. This is stuff that you, as a Nintendo fan myself, just never get to see.”
paragraphs.cn: Phillips分享说：「我一直刷新那个页面，想看从里面拼出的最新东西。你几乎永远看不到产品是怎么做出来的。所以Gigaleak令人兴奋之处就在这里：看到进行中的版本，看到被留在剪辑室地板上的东西。作为任天堂粉丝，这些是你平常根本看不到的。」

paragraphs.en: The Gigleak was a goldmine for any Nintendo fan. But once word got out that there was source code for an early build of Super Mario 64, Glitchy and co immediately got to work.
paragraphs.cn: Gigaleak对任何任天堂粉丝来说都是金矿。但一旦有消息传出，其中包含《超级马力欧64》早期版本的源码，Glitchy和其他人立刻开始行动。

paragraphs.en: “ So I checked the source file. There were no raw images, but I noticed something.”
paragraphs.cn: 「所以我检查了源文件。里面没有原始图片，但我注意到了一件东西。」

paragraphs.en: Glitchy immediately recognised the code language they’d used in modding tools for fan-made Nintendo 64 projects. The file structure was similar, so it made sense that they could find a way to dive deeper.
paragraphs.cn: Glitchy立刻认出了自己在粉丝自制Nintendo 64项目Mod工具中用过的代码语言。文件结构很相似，所以他们能找到办法深入挖掘也是合理的。

paragraphs.en: “Maybe with the tools, people can recreate stuff,” thought Glitchy. “I already had experience with textures.
paragraphs.cn: Glitchy当时想：「也许有了这些工具，人们就能重建一些东西。我已经有纹理方面的经验。」

paragraphs.en: “I made a little program to take the raw data that was in textures, defined byte by byte. It would take that and convert it to a PNG. I showed this to people in Discord, and they were like, ‘Oh damn, maybe we can find more texture this way.'”
paragraphs.cn: 「我做了一个小程序，用来读取纹理中的原始数据，也就是逐字节定义的数据。它会读取这些数据并转换成PNG。我把它展示给Discord里的人，他们就说，『哦该死，也许我们能用这种方法找到更多纹理。』」

paragraphs.en: Not only did they discover more textures in the source code, but Glitchy found something that would change the history of the L is Real movement forever. A texture of a white circle, with a green L.
paragraphs.cn: 他们不仅在源码里发现了更多纹理，Glitchy还找到了一样东西，永远改变了「L is Real」运动的历史：一张白色圆形纹理，上面有一个绿色的L。

paragraphs.en: “I don't want to toot my own horn, but it is probably true that I was the first person [to find it],” he says.
paragraphs.cn: 他说：「我不想自吹自擂，但我很可能确实是第一个找到它的人。」

paragraphs.en: The proof, although obtained by nefarious means, had finally arrived. Modders immediately got to work finding other elements and eventually pieced Luigi together in Super Mario 64. Now, although it was never released by Nintendo, there was evidence of a version of the classic game featuring Luigi.
paragraphs.cn: 证据终于到来了，尽管它是通过不光彩手段获得的。Mod作者们立刻开始寻找其他元素，最终把路易吉拼进了《超级马力欧64》。现在，尽管任天堂从未发布过这个版本，但确实有证据表明，经典游戏曾经存在过一个包含路易吉的版本。

paragraphs.en: Even though the sign in Peach’s courtyard never actually said "L Is Real 2401," the message serendipitously came true. Luigi’s textures were discovered on July 25, 2020, 24 years and 1 month after Mario 64’s original Japanese release.
paragraphs.cn: 尽管桃花公主庭院里的牌子实际上从未写着「L Is Real 2401」，这条信息却机缘巧合地成真了。路易吉的纹理于2020年7月25日被发现，距离《马力欧64》日本原版发售正好过去24年又1个月。
